questions.q: Of which island kingdom is Ravana the king in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana rules over the golden island kingdom of Lanka in the Ramayana.

questions.q: For how many heads is Ravana most famously depicted in tradition?
questions.explanation: Ravana is renowned as the ten-headed king, often called Dashagriva or Dashanana.

questions.q: To which class of beings does Ravana belong in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana is the mighty king of the rakshasas, a race of powerful demonic beings.

questions.q: Which queen does Ravana abduct, setting the central conflict of the Ramayana in motion?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducts Sita, the wife of Rama, which brings about the great war and his eventual fall.

questions.q: Which deity does Ravana revere as his supreme lord and devotedly worship?
questions.explanation: Ravana is celebrated in the texts as a great devotee of Shiva.

questions.q: At whose hands does Ravana ultimately meet his death in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana is slain by Rama on the battlefield of Lanka, fulfilling the purpose of Vishnu's avatar.

questions.q: In which epic is Ravana the principal antagonist?
questions.explanation: Ravana is the chief adversary of the Ramayana, the epic composed by the sage Valmiki.

questions.q: Who is the chief queen and consort of Ravana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Mandodari is the principal queen of Ravana, remembered for her wisdom and virtue.

questions.q: Which valiant younger brother of Ravana, famed for his immense size and prolonged sleep, fights alongside him as a great rakshasa warrior?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna, famed for his immense size and prolonged sleep, fights for his brother Ravana.

questions.q: Which righteous brother of Ravana abandons Lanka to side with Rama?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's virtuous brother, joins Rama and is later crowned king of Lanka.

questions.q: Who is the father of Ravana in the lineage given in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana is the son of the sage Vishrava and the rakshasa princess Kaikasi.

questions.q: Which mind-born sage of Brahma is the paternal grandfather of Ravana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana's father Vishrava was the son of the sage Pulastya, one of Brahma's mind-born sons, giving Ravana a brahmin descent.

questions.q: What is the original name by which Ravana was known before Shiva renamed him?
questions.explanation: He was first called Dashagriva, the ten-necked one, before gaining the name Ravana.

questions.q: From which deity does Ravana win his great boon of near-invincibility through severe austerities?
questions.explanation: Pleased by Ravana's harsh penance, Brahma grants him a boon of protection against gods and demons.

questions.q: Which class of beings did Ravana neglect to name in his boon, leaving him vulnerable to Rama?
questions.explanation: Scorning humans as too weak to harm him, Ravana omitted men from his boon, which allowed Rama the man to slay him.

questions.q: Who is Ravana's sister, whose disfigurement by Lakshmana inflames the conflict in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Surpanakha, Ravana's sister, is insulted in the forest, and her complaint leads Ravana to abduct Sita.

questions.q: Which rakshasa takes the form of a golden deer to lure Rama away before Sita's abduction?
questions.explanation: At Ravana's command, the rakshasa Maricha becomes a golden deer to draw Rama from Sita's side.

questions.q: Which noble vulture is mortally wounded while trying to stop Ravana from carrying off Sita?
questions.explanation: The aged vulture king Jatayu battles Ravana to rescue Sita and is fatally struck in the attempt.

questions.q: Within which grove in Lanka does Ravana hold Sita captive in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana confines Sita in the Ashoka Vatika, a grove of ashoka trees within his city.

questions.q: Which musical instrument is Ravana traditionally said to have played as a master devotee of Shiva?
questions.explanation: Ravana is celebrated as an accomplished veena player and is associated with the hymn of praise to Shiva.

questions.q: Which celestial flying chariot did Ravana seize and use, according to the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana took the flying chariot Pushpaka from his half-brother Kubera, the lord of wealth.

questions.q: From which half-brother did Ravana wrest the kingdom and city of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana drove out his half-brother Kubera, the god of wealth, and took Lanka and the Pushpaka chariot for himself.

questions.q: What is the name of Ravana's mighty son who defeats and binds Rama and Lakshmana with the serpent-arrows?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, is Ravana's powerful son who twice nearly overcomes Rama and Lakshmana in battle.

questions.q: Why is Ravana's son Meghanada honored with the title Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Meghanada earned the name Indrajit, conqueror of Indra, after defeating and capturing the king of the gods.

questions.q: When Ravana once lifted Mount Kailash, who pressed it down with a toe to humble him?
questions.explanation: When Ravana tried to uproot Kailash, Shiva pinned the mountain with his toe, trapping Ravana until he sang Shiva's praises.

questions.q: Which hymn of praise to Shiva is traditionally attributed to Ravana?
questions.explanation: The Shiva Tandava Stotra, an ecstatic hymn extolling Shiva's cosmic dance, is ascribed to Ravana.

questions.q: Who was the rakshasa mother of Ravana in the Ramayana lineage?
questions.explanation: Ravana was born to the sage Vishrava and the rakshasa princess Kaikasi, daughter of Sumali.

questions.q: According to a tradition in the Bhagavata Purana, Ravana was a rebirth of which gatekeeper of Vishnu's abode?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Ravana was one of Vishnu's doorkeepers, Jaya and Vijaya, cursed to be born as a demon.

questions.q: In a popular retelling of the Ramayana, where in Ravana's body does Vibhishana reveal that a reserve of life-nectar lies, which Rama must strike?
questions.explanation: In a widely told tradition, Vibhishana discloses that a store of amrita lay in Ravana's navel, which Rama dries up with his arrow to bring about his fall.

questions.q: Which divine weapon does Rama finally use to kill Ravana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama looses the Brahmastra, the weapon of Brahma given to him by the sage Agastya, to bring down Ravana.
